### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/d-matrix_ai-inference-agenticai-activity-7462577438842155008-tDhs
...
Activity: Jesse Harmon reacted on this
...
Agentic coding is changing software engineering fast. But the infrastructure behind it is hitting a wall.
...
As AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor drive increasingly complex workflows, GPU-only systems struggle with latency, efficiency, and scaling demands.
...
Heterogeneous and disaggregated inference pipelines offer a different path forward:
...
• Lower latency for interactive AI workflows
• Better utilization across memory-bound and compute-bound tasks
...
• Speculative decoding to accelerate complex agentic flows
• More efficient scaling for enterprise AI coding environments
...
The future of AI coding won’t rely on a single monolithic system. It will require purpose-built inference infrastructure designed for agentic workloads.
...
Read the latest from d-Matrix: https://lnkd.in/etK5EECM #AI #Inference #AgenticAI #GenerativeAI #LLM #AIInfrastructure #SpeculativeDecoding #MachineLearning #DeveloperTools
...
Activity: Jesse Harmon
...
### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/deepsheth3_machinelearning-recommendationsystems-activity-7454633070495928320--88c
...
Activity: Jesse Harmon reacted on this
...
I built a KV cache compression engine that serves 104,000 users per second from a single GPU.
...
Benchmarked on real Criteo ad data. On real NVIDIA hardware. With real profiling.
...
The problem: LLM-based recommendation systems run out of GPU memory fast. Each user's context lives in a KV cache, and at BF16 precision, you can only fit a few thousand concurrent users per GPU. More users = more GPUs = millions in infrastructure costs.
...
I built OmniStack-RS to fix this. It compresses KV caches 3.37x using a fused INT4+QJL codec: getting BF16 down to 4.75 bits per element with practically zero quality loss (max error vs FP32: 0.0024).
...
What that means in practice: 3.37x more users per GPU. 3.37x lower serving costs.
...
Results on Criteo Day 23 data (NVIDIA A10): 
• P99 latency: 0.69 ms (144x under MLPerf's 100ms deadline) 
• Throughput: 1,634 queries/sec, 104,571 users/sec 
• Compression: 3.37x (beat the 3.2x design target) 
• Numerical parity vs FP32: PASS
...
Under the hood: 
• INT4 Lloyd-Max quantization + 1-bit Rademacher QJL residual 
• Fused Triton attention kernel (dequant, softmax, and output in one pass) 
• O(1) Multi-LoRA dispatch for per-user personalization 
• Profiled with NVIDIA Nsight Compute (not just Python timers)
...
Built from scratch. No CUDA C, just pure Python and Triton. Looking to take these hardware-native optimizations to the next level: let's talk infra.
...
Code and benchmark proofs: https://lnkd.in/gyQZUvcj #MachineLearning #RecommendationSystems #GPUComputing #LLM #InfrastructureEngineering
...
Activity: Jesse Harmon reacted

Headquartered in Little Rock, Arkansas, United States, operates in 30 countries (including Panama, Canada, France, Colombia, and Iran). Has a $5.4B market cap. IPO 1969.

results.title: Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
highlights:
Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart
...
# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact
...
About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.
...
- Build and deploy production ML systems end-to-end: data pipelines, feature stores, model training, serving layers, monitoring, and feedback loops at scale
...
- Provide technical visionand lead advanced development in agentic AI, reinforcement learning, and simulation.
...
- Architect and deploy large-scale AI systems and autonomous AI agents — conversational assistants, predictive agents with tool-calling, and multi-agent orchestration systems using LangChain, Pydantic AI, and RAG patterns
...
- Deploy NLP pipelines at enterprise scale — semantic similarity across millions of records using BERT/SBERT embeddings and vector search (FAISS)
...
- Deep expertise in ML algorithms (XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, AutoML) and deep learning (PyTorch, transformer architectures).
...
- Production experience with NLP (BERT, SBERT, FAISS, RAG), computer vision (YOLO, CLIP), and time series forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet).
...
- Hands-on experience building and orchestrating multiple AI agents with LangChain, RAG, tool integration, and memory management.
...
- Multi-cloud ML platform proficiency — AWS SageMaker AND GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery, BigQuery ML).
...
- Strong Python skills (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) with containerization (Docker) and MLOps practices.
...
- Masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field AND 8+ years’ experience in data science or Machine Learning roles with demonstrated impact.
...
- PhD in AI, Machine Learning, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Econometrics, or related field with relevant industry experience.

## Job description
...
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
...
As a Software Engineer on the Shopping Ads team, you will be an "AI First" engineer dedicated to solving complex issues using Generative AI . You will design, implement, and launch innovative Large Language Model (LLM) and Machine Learning (ML) systems to enhance the shopping journey for users worldwide.

...

...

...

...

...

- Knowledge of designing large scale AI solutions, data integration, cleansing, and transformation techniques.
...
- Familiarity with BigData platforms and technologies, such as Hadoop or Spark.
...
- Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to think creatively and provide innovative solutions.
...
- Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively in multidisciplinary teams.
...
- Knowledge of ethical AI practices and regulations is a plus.
